summary: Tournament evaluation is the bounded selection arena for Gate D: finite, deterministic, replay-aware, rollback-bound, and still blocked from public showability or router cutover.

tournament_registry.purpose: Candidate must beat or bound against the static baseline under governed conditions.
Selection under pressure conditions already defined by R1.
No-regression protection against capability or behavior loss.
Adversarial and safety gating before later router eligibility.

selection_rules: Every tournament plan must be schema-bound and deterministic.
Only adapters already ELIGIBLE under the R2 lifecycle law may enter tournaments.
Tournament outcomes are admissible only when replayable receipt and rollback evidence exist where required.
Public showability and competition claims remain blocked even when the bounded tournament arena is ratified.

showability_boundary.rule_text: Tournament execution may be lawfully ratified while public tournament showability remains blocked.

claim_ceiling: R3 binds only the bounded selection arena for tournaments, promotion, and merge. It does not authorize router cutover, learned-router superiority, lobe activation, externality, comparative, or commercial widening.
